task_completion.description: Search the correct index for SSH events
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk search bar should contain a query starting with or including 'index="security_logs"' or 'index=security_logs'.

task_completion.description: Use the transaction command to group events by source IP
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SPL query in the search bar or panel configuration must contain the '| transaction' command, likely followed by an IP-related field.

task_completion.description: Save the search as a report named 'SSH_Brute_Force_Sessions'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message showing the report 'SSH_Brute_Force_Sessions' was saved, or the report visible in the Splunk 'Reports' tab.
A dashboard titled 'SSH_Session_Analysis' being viewed, edited, or listed in the Splunk 'Dashboards' tab.

task_completion.description: Add at least two visualization panels to the dashboard
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'SSH_Session_Analysis' dashboard must display at least two distinct panels (e.g., a table of longest sessions and a chart of events per session) populated with data.

integrity.description: The agent used the Splunk Web UI in a browser
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Splunk Enterprise web interface (http://localhost:8000) in a web browser, not terminal curl commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The dashboard panels display actual Splunk data
integrity.visual_evidence: The tables and charts in the dashboard should show realistic data (e.g., IP addresses, event counts, durations) rather than being empty, mocked, or showing error messages.

integrity.description: The agent did not just write the SPL query in a text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: The SPL queries and configurations must be entered into the actual Splunk interface, not just typed into a notepad or terminal text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target index is 'security_logs'. The SPL query must explicitly use the 'transaction' command to group events. The saved report must be named 'SSH_Brute_Force_Sessions'. The dashboard must be named 'SSH_Session_Analysis' and contain at least two visualization panels.
